title: solanum melongena var. insanum
intro: Solanum melongena var. insanum, commonly known as the bitter eggplant or devil's eggplant, is a variety of the eggplant plant that is characterized by its bitter and toxic properties. The plant contains solanine, a glycoalkaloid compound with medicinal properties, which has been used to treat various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Solanum melongena var. insanum include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as heart disease and cancer. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made from the leaves and stems, which can be dried and used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions.
